LOCALITY: High View Trail, Burnsville, North Carolina, US (35.842, -82.159)
Mt. Mitchell SE Mount Mitchell Trail, Burnsville, North Carolina, US (35.757, -82.253)
SPECIES COMMENTS: Viewed from above, gliding ~200 yards beneath me along briefly following the brushy powerline cut before cutting right, following the ridgeline but travelling in the canopy through the tall red spruces, gliding no higher than 30ft through the forest. I waited 20 minutes as the sun set in hopes of it reemerging, but it likely either continued along the slopeline or perched out of sight under the canopy.-Strikingly large and bulky, with broad wings and longer tail. Gray plumage from above. Views of head plumage were poor. Significantly greater in size and broader winged than a coopers hawk to me, impression closer to a large buteo.-Goshawk is extremely scarce this far south and in general. I would of course  prefer to have pics for such a scarcity, however the details written are as seen and I feel confident in goshawk from the behavior, size and profile, and habitat.-This area has been battered by large winter storms coming in on northern winds for over the whole past week, and the weather today was no exception. It was about 10f° when I observed this hawk, and with extremely strong winds. I personally couldn't picture a more regular raptor species sticking it out up this high in the conditions.-I welcome any feedback or thoughts on this report.
